question: Does the paper report HIV sequences from patient samples?
explain: The paper focuses on in vitro resistance selection studies using HIV-1 subtype A, B, and C viruses in cell culture systems. It does not mention obtaining HIV sequences from patient samples.
sentences: "In vitro resistance selection of subtype B virus (MT4-green fluorescent protein [GFP] cells), as well as subtype A and C viruses (MT4-GFP/CCR5 cells) was conducted with DOR, rilpivirine (RPV), and efavirine (EFV) under low-multiplicity-of-infection conditions in a 96-well format."; "The cells used (293T) were from the ATCC (Manassas, VA)."; "Each mutant was created by the site-directed mutagenesis method via gene synthesis and subcloning into plasmid RT112, which contained full-length R8 provirus DNA."; "Cells (293T) were grown in Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium containing 10% FBS."; "MT4-GFP/CCR5 cells were generated by introducing stably expressed CCR5 genes into MT4-GFP cells."

question: Does the paper report in vitro drug susceptibility data?
explain: The paper includes data on the susceptibility of HIV-1 subtype B mutant viruses selected by DOR, RPV, and EFV to NNRTIs. It discusses the resistance levels of various mutants to these drugs.
sentences: "The susceptibility of subtype B mutant viruses selected by DOR, RPV, and EFV to NNRTIs was evaluated."; "Results suggest that mutant viruses selected by DOR are susceptible to RPV and EFV and mutants selected by RPV and EFV are susceptible to DOR."; "DOR displays efficacy against the WT virus, as well as the two most prevalent NNRTI-associated mutant viruses containing K103N and Y181C substitutions, with EC95s of 20, 42, and 27 nM in the presence of 50% NHS, respectively."; "The susceptibilities of mutants selected by EFV to NNRTIs are shown in Fig. 3B."; "Figure 3C shows the susceptibilities of mutants selected by RPV to NNRTIs."

question: What were the GenBank accession numbers for sequenced HIV isolates?
explain: The paper does not provide any GenBank accession numbers for sequenced HIV isolates. It focuses on in vitro resistance selection and does not mention depositing sequences in GenBank.

question: Which HIV species were studied in the paper?
explain: The paper specifically mentions that Doravirine (DOR) is a nonnucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitor (NNRTI) for HIV-1 and discusses resistance selection with HIV-1 subtype A, B, and C viruses.
sentences: "Doravirine (DOR, formerly known as MK-1439) is a human immunodeficiency type 1 virus (HIV-1) nonnucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitor (NNRTI) that is currently in phase 2b clinical trials."; "In vitro resistance selection of subtype B virus (MT4-green fluorescent protein [GFP] cells), as well as subtype A and C viruses (MT4-GFP/CCR5 cells) was conducted with DOR, rilpivirine (RPV), and efavirine (EFV) under low-multiplicity-of-infection conditions in a 96-well format."

question: Which HIV genes were reported to have been sequenced?
explain: The paper focuses on the sequencing of the reverse transcriptase (RT) region of HIV-1. It mentions the amplification and sequencing of the RT-encoding region.
sentences: "The RT-encoding region was amplified by the one-step RT-PCR method."; "PCR products were genotyped by an automated population-based full-length sequencing method (covering amino acids 1 to 440 of the RT region)."; "The primers used for PCR amplification of subtype B virus were AAGCAGGAGCCGATAGACAA (forward) and TAATCCCGAATCCTGCAAAGCTAGA (reverse)."; "The sequencing primers were 5′-CCCTGTGGAAGCAC and 5′-GGATGTGGGCGATGC."

question: What method was used for sequencing?
explain: The paper mentions the use of the ABI 3100 analyzer and the SeqScape software V3, which are associated with Sanger sequencing.
sentences: "The SeqScape software V3 and ABI 3100 analyzer used were from Applied Biosystems (Foster City, CA)."; "The RT-encoding region was amplified by the one-step RT-PCR method."; "PCR products were genotyped by an automated population-based full-length sequencing method (covering amino acids 1 to 440 of the RT region)."

question: Were samples cloned prior to sequencing?
explain: The paper mentions that RT-PCR products were cloned into the TOPO TA vector and then transformed into Top10 competent cells for clonal sequence analysis.
sentences: "For clonal sequence analysis, RT-PCR products were cloned into the TOPO TA vector and then transformed into Top10 competent cells; 30 bacterial colonies from each sample were analyzed."
